name: Cranberry-Orange Biscotti
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Spray a large or two small cookie sheets with nonstick cooking spray.
3. In a large bowl, combine sugar and brown sugar; beat until well blended.
4. Add margarine and mix until combined.
5. Stir in orange peel and eggs; beat well.
6. Lightly spoon flour into measuring cup; level off. Stir in flour and baking powder; mix well.
7. Stir in cranberries and almonds.
8. Shape dough into three 7-inch long rolls.
9. Place rolls at least 3 inches apart on the prepared cookie sheet; flatten each to form a rectangle that is about 3/4 inch thick, 3 inches wide, and 7 inches long.
10. Bake for 18-25 minutes or until golden brown and firm to the touch. Let cool on wire racks for 5 minutes.
11. Transfer rectangles to a baking sheet; bake an additional 6-8 minutes or until top surface is slightly dry. Turn cookies over and bake another 6-8 minutes or until top surface is slightly dry.
12. Remove from oven and let cool completely on wire racks.
13. In a small bowl, whisk together powdered sugar, remaining orange peel, milk, and enough to reach desired drizzling consistency.
14. Drizzle glaze over cooled cookies; let stand until set.
15. Store in an airtight container. Makes about 4 dozen cookies. Each cookie contains approximately 80 calories, with 20 calories from fat and 2 grams of fat per serving.
